issue_summary: Update edgesConnecting().size() and edgesConnecting().isEmpty() to be more efficient for Networks with parallel edges. In particular, take advantage of the cached successor multisets. Currently the methods are always O(D). This will also be O(D) if successors hasn't been invoked, but will be O(1) for future calls. For example, with something like
javadoc_before:
/** * A class to represent the set of edges connecting an (implicit) origin node to a target node. * *

The {@link #nodeToEdgeMap} means this class only works on networks without parallel edges. * * @author James Sexton * @param Edge parameter type */
javadoc_after:
/** * A class to represent the set of edges connecting an (implicit) origin node to a target node. * *

The {@link #nodeToOutEdge} map means this class only works on networks without parallel edges. * See {@link MultiEdgesConnecting} for a class that works with parallel edges. * * @author James Sexton * @param Edge parameter type */
